description_long: This object is a short sword with a straight, double-edged blade that tapers to a pointed tip. The blade is metallic and has a uniform, silvery-gray finish, suggesting it is made of steel or a similar metal. The crossguard is simple and rectangular, providing basic hand protection, and is also metallic. The handle is cylindrical, wrapped in a brownish material that appears to be either leather or textured wood, and is capped with a square pommel. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible ornamentation or decorative features. The proportions and construction suggest it is a functional weapon, likely intended for close combat or display.
description: A straight, double-edged short sword with a metallic blade, simple crossguard, and a brown-wrapped cylindrical handle capped by a square pommel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the straight blade, crossguard, and the cylindrical handle with a square pommel.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the blade and handle, with the crossguard and pommel visible as narrow rectangles.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the straight blade, crossguard, and handle details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the blade and handle, similar to view 1.
